---
title: "Andreas Filser"
pagetitle: "About"
subtitle: Sociologist & Data Scientist<br>FDZ-IAB
image: ../assets/avatar.jpg
about:
  template: trestles
  image-width: 12em
  image-shape: rounded
  id: hero-heading
  links:
    - text: "{{< fa brands bluesky >}}"
      aria-label: "Bluesky"
      href: https://bsky.app/profile/filsa.bsky.social
    - text: "{{< fa link >}}"
      aria-label: "Employer Website (IAB)"
      href: https://iab.de/mitarbeiter/?id=5370712
    - text: "{{< ai google-scholar >}}"
      aria-label: "Google Scholar"
      href: https://scholar.google.de/citations?user=vP7topAAAAAJ
    - icon: github
      aria-label: GitHub
      href: https://github.com/filius23
    - text: "{{< ai orcid >}}"
      href: https://orcid.org/0000-0001-5530-3621
    - icon: linkedin
      aria-label: "LinkedIn"
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/andreas-filser-85919450/
    - text: "{{< ai osf >}}"
      aria-label: "OSF"
      href: https://osf.io/m7nec/

format: 
  html: 
    page-layout: full

header-includes: >
  <link rel="stylesheet" href="about.css">
resources:
  - about.css
---

::: {#hero-heading}
<span class="h1">Hello and Willkommen! 👋</span>

I am a sociologist and demographer at the Research Data Centre of the Institute for Employment Research ([FDZ-IAB](https://fdz.iab.de/en.aspx)) in Nuremberg. 

My research agenda spans two broader areas: the gendered consequences of parenthood for labour market outcomes, specifically, a comparative France-Germany project on mothers' employment trajectories and ongoing work on fathers' parental leave. Additonally, rooted in my dissertation, I am interested the social consequences of imbalanced sex ratios for relationship formation, risk-taking, and violence.

I also regularly teach [courses](/courses) on applied statistics and programming in R and Stata – for social scientists across all levels, from undergraduate students to PhD researchers and beyond, in German and English.
My teaching is informed by hands-on experience with reproducible research workflows – including version control, clean code, and documented pipelines – gained through my work with large-scale administrative data at the FDZ-IAB.


<!-- I am a sociologist and demographer at the Research Data Centre of the Institute for Employment Research ([FDZ-IAB](https://fdz.iab.de/en.aspx)) in Nuremberg (Germany).  -->
  
<!-- My research focuses upon how couples navigate the labour market when transitioning into parenthood. Currently, I am part of an international project where we analyse the employment trajectories of mothers in France and Germany. Another current project explores the determinants and consequences of fathers' parental leave. -->

<!-- I enjoy the linkage, procurement, and analysis of data using R and Stata. In a recent [paper](/publication/2022_update_identifying_mothers/), we updated the identification process of mothers in the labour market data at the IAB. Another [paper]() provides smartphone-based mobility indicators for PASS participations. Currently, I am developing an [R-shiny](https://shiny.posit.co/) dashboard to streamline data applications at the FDZ-IAB. -->

<!-- Moreover, I am interested in consequences of imbalanced numbers of women and men in groups or regions, i.e. imbalanced sex ratios. In my dissertation project, I analysed how skewed sex ratios are associated with the formation of relationships, risk-taking, and violence. -->

<!-- Beyond research, I enjoy teaching applied statistics and programming using R and Stata. Find a collection of course materials [here](/courses). -->
:::
## About me
::: {.grid}
::: {.g-col-6 #about-me-1}
<!-- I enjoy all things data - from teaching undergraduate and PhD-level statistics and programming courses to provisioning data and tinkering with {{< fa brands r-project >}}  and Stata. -->

I design and deliver courses on applied statistics and programming for social scientists – covering everything from data wrangling and visualization to regression and reproducible workflows. If you are looking for a dedicated instructor, feel free to [reach out](/contact) – I'd be happy to collaborate!

:::
::: {.g-col-6 #about-me-2}
::: {.edu-item}
::: {.edu-icon}
{{< fa graduation-cap >}}
:::
::: {.edu-text}
PhD in Social Sciences (Dr. phil.)

::: {.edu-detail}
University of Oldenburg · 2022
:::
:::
:::
::: {.edu-item}
::: {.edu-icon}
{{< fa graduation-cap >}}
:::
::: {.edu-text}
M.Sc. in Socioeconomics

::: {.edu-detail}
Friedrich-Alexander-University Erlangen-Nuremberg · 2016
:::
:::
:::
::: {.edu-item}
::: {.edu-icon}
{{< fa graduation-cap >}}
:::
::: {.edu-text}
B.A. in Sociology & Economics

::: {.edu-detail}
University of Konstanz · 2012
:::
:::
:::
:::
:::
<!-- end grid -->

## 
:::: {.grid}
::: {.g-col-3}
#### [Publications](../publications){.about-links .subtitle}
:::
::: {.g-col-3}
#### [Teaching](../teaching){.about-links .subtitle}
:::
::: {.g-col-3}
#### [CV](../cv){.about-links .subtitle}
:::
::: {.g-col-3}
#### [Blog (under construction)](../blog){.about-links .subtitle}
:::
::::
